reasoning: The Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases" lists Coccidioidomycosis with a blank entry for "Vaccine" as of January 5, 2026. A search for Coccidioidomycosis vaccines (Valley fever) indicates that the development of a human vaccine is currently funded but still only in early/pre-clinical stages (Dr. Galgiani, November 2025; Anivive at the World Vaccine Congress, May 2025). There is no approved human vaccine. While Anivive Lifesciences is close to getting USDA approval for a *canine* Valley fever vaccine (potentially around "early 2026"), it has not yet been approved as of late 2025, and obtaining human FDA approval is completely distinct and years away. Furthermore, an approval of a canine vaccine would be highly unlikely to rapidly trigger a Wikipedia editor to update a broad human-centric "List of infectious diseases" page's vaccine column to "Yes" within the tiny window of Jan 5–Feb 17, 2026. Thus, I assign 2% for Jan 25, 2026, and 3% for Feb 17, 2026, given the minimal chance of a sudden animal vaccine approval paired with an aberrant Wikipedia editorial update or an unannounced major human vaccine breakthrough.
